page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading, operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, which translates to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 600 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is categorized in the bottom 10%, indicating a limitation for single-threaded tasks. Similarly, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks, both read and write, is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation with Clang fall into the middle 50% average tier. Cryptographic operations, assisted by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instruction sets, also achieve average performance.

Given its hardware profile, the c5ad.4xlarge offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated AMD EPYC cores and fast local NVMe storage without requiring high memory-to-vCPU ratios. It is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and intermediate software compilation. However, it is less suitable for memory-intensive applications or single-threaded tasks due to its memory bandwidth and single-core performance constraints.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes a 600 GB local NVMe SSD and a 5 Gbps baseline network speed. While single-core CPU performance is poor and large-block memory bandwidth is weak, the instance delivers average multi-core CPU, database, and web serving performance. This makes it a qualitatively cost-effective option for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and applications requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 600 GB NVMe SSD, offering average multi-core performance but weak single-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 600 GB NVMe SSD for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, fast local NVMe SSD storage, and balanced multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor at 3.3 GHz
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Average multi-core CPU, database, and web serving benchmark performance
Weak single-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth
Best suited for multi-threaded batch processing, web serving, and software compilation
